monument_name: Baba Banda Singh Bahadur War Memorial
description: A modern architectural marvel commemorating the victory of Sikh forces at the Battle of Chappar Chiri.

A robust hilltop fort built by the Vijayanagara Empire, offering panoramic views of the region.

Massive, intricately carved ancient wooden gateways that historically protected the Chümoukedima village from invaders.
Colossal, ancient hollowed-out wooden log drums housed in massive structural Morungs (traditional dormitories).
Ancient natural caves that were structurally modified by early Naga tribes into impregnable defensive fortresses.
Scattered, mysterious mushroom-shaped stone pillars belonging to the ancient 10th-century Kachari civilization.
Historic, ancient structural furnaces and blacksmithing sites where the Khiamniungan tribe forged their legendary weapons.
Exceptionally well-preserved, centuries-old traditional Zeliangrong Naga wooden youth dormitories decorated with animal skulls.
A legendary ancient flat stone (Tso Tawo) that early Naga tribes believed magically multiplied the grain laid upon it.
A highly significant collection of ancient megalithic stones erected to celebrate the headhunting victories of the Yimkhiung tribe.
Massive prehistoric stone pillars erected by the Rengma Nagas to honor feasts of merit and legendary ancestors.
The ancient, structural foundation site believed to be the mythical birthplace of the Chang Naga tribe.

Historic, elaborately carved traditional fortresses and dormitories representing the martial heritage of the Sumi Nagas.
A deeply moving structural monument honoring the incredible bravery of Indian soldiers during the 1962 Sino-Indian War.
A massive, recently unearthed historic cemetery containing the graves of over 1,000 Allied soldiers from World War II.
Ancient structural remnants of early Idu Mishmi tribal settlements hidden in the deep, remote Himalayan valleys.
A ruined historic military fort that once guarded the crucial Himalayan trade routes to Tibet.
A legendary rock-cliff fort where the Adi tribes famously mounted a fierce resistance against the British in 1911.
Ancient, elaborate structural bamboo and stone altars erected by the Nyishi tribe for traditional Donyi-Polo worship.
One of the oldest and most architecturally significant timber churches in the deeply remote Nyishi tribal region.
A terrifyingly high, historic structural cane-and-wood suspension bridge built by indigenous tribes centuries ago.
Ancient megalithic arrangements and historical boundary markers erected by the Galo tribe.
A spectacular Tibetan Buddhist monastery built by refugees, featuring highly ornate architecture and a golden Buddha.
Magnificent, ancient traditional dormitories featuring spectacular, intricate wood carvings of warriors and skulls.
An incredibly significant 14th-century archaeological site featuring the intricately carved stone ruins of an ancient Hindu temple.
Massive, ancient wooden structural platforms that serve as the historic socio-cultural centerpieces of Apatani villages.
An ornate, historic Tai-Khamti Buddhist pagoda featuring stunning Burmese-style architecture.
Historic tribal administrative outposts from the early NEFA (North-East Frontier Agency) era.
A breathtaking, 400-year-old Mahayana Buddhist monastery perched on a hilltop in the remote Mechuka valley.
The structural remains of an ambitious early 20th-century suspension bridge constructed by the British over the Siang river.

An imposing, vividly painted ancient Tibetan Buddhist monastery located just miles from the Chinese border.
A marvel of ancient tribal engineering, a massive historic bridge woven entirely from local cane and bamboo.
A highly sacred structural temple built around a natural waterfall, believed to be the site of a mythological Hindu event.
A beautiful, historic masonry church representing the deep Christian architectural footprint in the Naga borderlands.
A deeply significant structural monument and park commemorating the indigenous leaders of the Kuki-Zomi tribes.
A historic, colonial-era suspension bridge that served as the primary trade gateway between Manipur and Assam.
A complex of intricately built modern and historic temples situated on a hillock, featuring distinct Meitei architecture.

A deeply revered, ancient structural shrine perched atop one of the highest peaks in Manipur.
Historic structural remnants, trenches, and defensive positions from a fierce WWII battle between the British and Japanese.
The ruined structural remains of a strategic 19th-century colonial outpost located at the confluence of two massive rivers.
A legendary heritage site featuring ancient megaliths and a sacred tree, believed to be the dispersal point of all Naga tribes.
Ancient, 2000-year-old rock-cut carvings and structural modifications made by the indigenous Hobinian culture.

Historic, colonial-style wooden bungalows built in the 20th century, surrounded by one of the oldest tea estates in the state.
A spectacular, ancient Buddhist shrine known for its massive prayer wheels and classic Sikkimese timber architecture.
A historic, brightly painted Nyingma Buddhist monastery that serves as the spiritual heart of the local Lepcha community.
A breathtakingly beautiful, 18th-century Kagyu monastery rebuilt multiple times, famous for its ancient murals and frescoes.
A ruined hilltop fort standing at 1,980 meters, once the summer residence of Raja Bijai Chand.
A magnificent complex of 84 ancient stone temples built in the 9th century by the local kings.
A historic 18th-century fort built by Raja Abhay Chand, famous for its Katoch dynasty wall paintings.
One of the oldest and largest forts in the Himalayas, tracing its origins back to the ancient Trigarta Kingdom.
An ancient, tower-like wooden fort situated at an altitude of 2,600 meters, housing an idol of Kamakhya Devi.
A unique 16th-century wooden temple with a pagoda-shaped roof, dedicated to the wife of Bhima from the Mahabharata.
A spectacular 11th-century Tibetan Buddhist monastery perched on a hilltop at an altitude of 4,166 meters.
A 13th-century pagoda-style temple built entirely from a single deodar tree, situated beside a floating-island lake.

A historic fort built by Gurkha leader Ranjor Singh Thapa in 1810 using material recovered from the Nahan fort.
An 800-year-old fort featuring freshwater springs and exquisite Rajput-style architectural details.

A historic fort built in 1733, later repurposed as a prison for freedom fighters.

A majestic 18th-century fort that has witnessed the reign of the Dogras, Sikhs, and local Muslim rulers.
Spectacular 9th-century ruins of twin Hindu temples built by King Awantivarman of the Utpala dynasty.
A 19th-century fort built by Mian Hathu on a hillock overlooking the Rajouri town.
A historic and difficult-to-access fort perched atop a mountain, once a stronghold of the local kings.
An impressive hill fort that was reinforced with stone by Maharaja Gulab Singh, serving as a royal treasury.
An 18th-century fort situated on the banks of the Basantar River, built by the Dogra rulers.
A historic Mughal caravanserai built along the ancient Mughal Road by Emperor Jahangir.

A complex of 11th-century ancient stone temples featuring Hellenic influences, believed to date back to the Mahabharata.
An enormous 9-meter tall rock-carved statue of the Maitreya Buddha dating back to the 8th century.

A UNESCO World Heritage site and the primary residence of the Mughal Emperors for nearly 200 years.
The magnificent 16th-century tomb that inspired the Taj Mahal, featuring perfect Mughal proportions.
A Gothic-style red sandstone tower built by the British to commemorate soldiers lost during the 1857 Rebellion.

A massive, ruined stone fortress built in 1321 by Ghiyas-ud-din Tughlaq, spanning over 6 km.
A lesser-known 17th-century hunting lodge and mini-minaret built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.
A UNESCO World Heritage site featuring the modernist architectural marvels designed by Le Corbusier.
A historic colonial prison in Port Blair where Indian political prisoners were exiled during the struggle for independence.
A 17th-century beautifully carved wooden mosque situated on Kavaratti Island.
A historic temple dedicated to a female Nayanar saint, uniquely blending Chola and French architectural influences.
The ruins of an ancient Roman trade center dating back to the 2nd century BCE.
A historic cemetery featuring elaborate obelisks and graves of French administrators from the 18th century.

description: A massive 16th-century colonial fort built by the Portuguese to guard their settlement.

description: A heavily fortified 16th-century Portuguese castle located strategically at the mouth of the Gulf of Khambhat.

A magnificent 14th-century Sufi shrine showcasing classic Kashmiri wooden and stone masonry architecture.

A historic obelisk surrounded by an iron fence commemorating the soldiers who fell during the 1803 Battle of Delhi.

The surviving colonial-era and late-Mughal havelis in the Chhota Bazar area of this historic city originally founded by Shah Jahan.

state: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
monument_name: Church of Our Lady of Piety
description: An exquisite stone church built in Silvassa in 1897, representing the stunning Portuguese architectural legacy in the region.
